_comment: Regression suite for the single `move` tool.
degrees absolute, 0=neutral. neck ±45 (neg=up, pos=down). foot ±90 (neg=left, pos=right).
Predicates: degRange (right amount), durRange (right speed), minSteps/maxSteps (length),
validEnums (valid joints), noTool/noMove (refuse-impossible). Windows are generous —
we test 'sensible', not exact. Cases are chosen to DISCRIMINATE.

cases.note: AMOUNT: 'all the way' → near the -45° limit. A timid -15° fails.

cases.prompt: tilt your head up just a tiny bit
cases.note: AMOUNT: 'tiny bit' → small negative, NOT a full swing.

cases.prompt: turn a little to the right
cases.note: AMOUNT: 'a little' → modest positive, not full 90.

cases.prompt: turn all the way to your left
look level and straight at me
cases.note: NEUTRAL anchor: 'level' = neck 0°. Sanity check the model uses 0 for neutral (centering the neck is the key signal).

cases.prompt: look up and turn to your right at the same time
cases.note: PARALLEL: 'at the same time' → neck + foot in ONE step's `parts`. maxSteps:1 forces a single simultaneous step, not two sequential ones.

cases.prompt: lower your head and swivel left together
cases.note: PARALLEL: both joints in one step. Discriminates models that split into two steps.
REPEAT: 5 nods ≈ 10 steps. Must NOT refuse ('I can only nod once'). Earlier the model declined to call the tool at all.

cases.prompt: shake your head no three times
cases.note: REPEAT: a head-shake is foot left/right; 3× = a long sequence.
SHORT sequence: a single nod = down then up = 2 steps. Lower bound of composition.

cases.prompt: look around the room slowly, checking left then right
cases.note: SEQUENCE + SLOW: foot left, pause, right, pause, center. 'slowly' → large duration_ms (≥800).

cases.prompt: act curious — tilt up to look at me, pause, then lean down to inspect something
cases.note: SEQUENCE + AMOUNT: up-tilt then down-lean with a beat. Needs both directions of control.
FAST: 'quickly snap' → small duration_ms (≤300) AND a real downward move.

cases.prompt: very slowly and gently look all the way up
cases.note: SLOW: 'very slowly/gently' → large duration_ms (≥1000) AND near full up.

cases.prompt: do a little dance — move your head and body around for a few seconds
cases.note: LONG/improvised: open-ended → a rich multi-step sequence. A weak model does one move or refuses.

cases.prompt: spin your head all the way around in a full circle
cases.note: HONESTY: a full 360° head spin is impossible (neck is ±45° pitch). The model must ACKNOWLEDGE it can't (keyword) — an expressive partial gesture alongside is fine/good, so we don't forbid movement, just require honesty in words.
NO MOVE: pure chat must not trigger body movement (an expressive set_face is fine).
